title: Korean-Style Beef Gimbap
description: A delicious and easy-to-make Korean dish featuring crispy, chewy rice and savory beef fillings. This recipe is perfect for a quick lunch or dinner.
instructions: Prepare the gimbap rice according to the package instructions for a crispy texture.
In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, brown sugar, and Korean chili flakes. Add the sliced beef and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat a large non-stick skillet or griddle over medium-high heat.
Remove the beef from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Cook the beef for 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Transfer the cooked beef to a cutting board and let rest for 5 minutes.
To assemble the gimbap, lay a sheet of gim (dried seaweed) flat and spread a thin layer of gimbap rice onto the center.
Add a few slices of the cooked beef on top of the rice, followed by a sprinkle of green onions.
Roll the gimbap tightly and slice into individual servings.
